/api/status (handleStatusRoute) — packages/web/src/server/routes-status.ts
  GET     /api/status
/api/token/ (handleTokenUsageRoute) — packages/web/src/server/routes-token-usage.ts
  GET     /api/token/budget
  GET     /api/token/by-command
  GET     /api/token/by-period
  GET     /api/token/events
  GET     /api/token/summary
/api/rex/validate (handleValidationRoute) — packages/web/src/server/routes-validation.ts
  GET     /api/rex/dependency-graph
  GET     /api/rex/validate
/api/hench/workflow/ (handleWorkflowRoute) — packages/web/src/server/routes-workflow.ts
  GET     /api/hench/workflow/analysis
  GET     /api/hench/workflow/history
  POST    /api/hench/workflow/apply
  POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id

</routes>

<findings>

[warning] Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects
[warning] 11 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [web-dashboard]
[warning] Verify that packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts has active consumers inside the hench package. Its presence in a low-cohesion residual zone may indicate it is imported by fewer modules than expected for a store-layer file. If consumer count is zero or one, consider merging it into an adjacent store file to strengthen the internal connectivity that keeps it within the hench-agent zone boundary. [hench-agent]
[warning] The gateway re-export-only contract is machine-enforced for web's gateways via domain-isolation.test.js, but hench's rex-gateway.ts has no equivalent enforcement test. The asymmetry means a logic-bearing re-export added to hench's gateway passes all CI checks undetected. Add a parallel gateway-isolation test inside the hench package or extend architecture-policy.test.js to cover all gateway files across all packages. [web-dashboard]
[warning] The MCP HTTP transport path (ndx start → /mcp/rex, /mcp/sourcevision endpoints) has no e2e test coverage; the stdio transport path is validated indirectly through delegation tests, creating an asymmetric risk as HTTP becomes the recommended transport. [cli-e2e-tests]
[warning] No integration test covers the hench→rex gateway contract; hench is the primary autonomous consumer of rex at runtime, yet rex-gateway.ts re-export stability is unvalidated in this zone — API drift between rex releases would go undetected until a hench runtime failure. [cross-package-integration-tests]
[warning] The 'web' residual zone (cohesion: 0.33, 14 files) co-existing with the 'web-dashboard' zone (cohesion: 0.98, 391 files) indicates incomplete zone membership for the web package — files stranded in the residual zone make health metrics for both zones unreliable. [web-dashboard]
[warning] architecture-policy.test.js and the cross-package-integration-tests zone together form complementary enforcement layers (static import rules + runtime wiring), but the gap between them — runtime gateway shape correctness — is uncovered by either zone. [cli-e2e-tests]
[warning] The two-file zone is disproportionately thin relative to the four active cross-package boundaries (hench→rex, web→rex, web→sourcevision, cli.js→all packages); as each boundary evolves independently the integration test gap widens. [cross-package-integration-tests]
[warning] rex-runtime-data acts as a shared-state bus between hench-agent, web-viewer, and monorepo-root via disk I/O rather than module imports. This file-based coupling is structurally invisible to Louvain zone detection and creates real concurrency hazards (documented in CLAUDE.md) that no import-graph metric can surface. [rex-runtime-data]
[warning] The architecture-policy test enforces gateway and tier-boundary rules via static source analysis but has no runtime assertion that gateway files contain only re-exports and no logic; a gateway that wraps a function with added behavior (e.g. logging, error transformation) passes all static checks while silently violating the re-export-only contract. [cli-e2e-tests]
[warning] The concurrency contract table in CLAUDE.md documents 9 unsafe command pairs, but scheduler-startup.test.js only validates server startup sequencing — no test exercises concurrent writes to .rex/prd.json or .sourcevision/ to verify data-corruption prevention; the contract is documentation-only with zero automated regression. [cross-package-integration-tests]
[warning] Two distinct Louvain communities are both assigned the zone ID 'hench-agent': the 155-file main zone (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0.0) and the 14-file cross-package residual (cohesion 0.33, coupling 0.67). Duplicate zone IDs make health metrics ambiguous — tooling that aggregates or thresholds by ID silently blends metrics from two structurally incompatible groups. The residual zone requires a distinct ID before its metrics can be acted upon. [hench-agent]
[warning] The config.js spawn-exempt rule (permitted to read JSON config files directly, forbidden from importing TypeScript package modules) has no automated enforcement. The architecture-policy e2e test verifies the four-tier import hierarchy generally, but config.js's specific exception boundary is untested — a package module import added to config.js would pass CI undetected. [monorepo-root]
[critical] prd.json is written by hench-agent (task status updates), monorepo-root (plan operations), and web MCP handlers without any file-level write coordinator. CLAUDE.md documents 'data corruption risk' for concurrent plan+work writes but provides no code-level mitigation — an advisory lock file or write-queue module is entirely absent. This is a missing abstraction layer for a shared mutable resource with multiple concurrent writers. [rex-runtime-data]
... +11 more

</findings>
